title: stylidium validum
intro: Stylidium validum, also known as the triggerplant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae. It is native to the southeastern regions of Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The triggerplant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, due to its rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. Stylidium validum is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and is traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat a range of ailments.
